semanticCore: Exemplar, role model, following, guide, pattern, or path
quranicRelevance: Following guidance of past prophets (Iqtadih - 'fabihudahumu iqtadih'), and following ancestors ('muqtadoona').

keyDerivations.meaning: To follow as a role model, imitate guidance, copy pattern, or follow exemplar

figurativeOrRareUsages: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللَّهُ ۖ فَبِهُدَاهُمُ اقْتَدِهْ (9:90): Those are the ones whom God guided, so follow/imitate their guidance (instruction to the Prophet).
discardedNoise: Cooking pots (Qidr) - spelling and root difference.
summaryEn: The root represents role models and guidance imitation (Iqtidaa'), ordering the Prophet to follow the path of past messengers, and mocking blind conformers.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التأسيس والاقتداء بالقدوة واتباع الأثر (قدوة)، آمراً الرسول باتباع هدى الأنبياء السابقين (اقتده)، وذاماً التقليد الأعمى للوثنية.
rawText: قَادِيَةٌ The first that come to one, or come upon one, of a company of men. (TA in art. طحم.) قُدْوَةٌ and قِدْوَةٌ (S, Msb, K,) and قَدْوَةٌ (K,) A pattern; an exemplar; an example; an object of imitation; one who is, or is to be, imitated. (S, Msb, K, TA.) See إِسْوَةٌ. قِدْيَهٌ : see فِدْيَهٌ.
